Morgoth slowing — evaluation inventory

Every evaluation the paper needs, with status + captioned results. Curate the checklist; ✓ = have it, ○ = planned. Companion to the ingestion burndown.

Paper evaluations — checklist (curate this)

Cohort & normative curves

✓Table 1 — cohort characteristics (age/sex/label; live-updating)
✓Growth curves: key features (rel δ, DAR, TAR, log δ) vs age
✓Growth curves: same features vs age, per sleep stage (W/N1/N2/N3/REM)
✓Feature-extraction validation (our Python vs prior features, r 0.89–0.95)
○Homologous-asymmetry norms vs age (for lateralization deviation)

Detection — whether / what

✓Abnormal-vs-normal AUROC, age-dependent (Morgoth)
✓Focal-vs-normal & generalized-vs-normal, age-dependent
✓Our deviation-LR vs Morgoth: agreement (r≈0.69) + distillation R²≈0.46
✓Report-flag agreement (Morgoth/our-LR vs clinical report flags)
✓ROC / PRC / calibration for abnormal detection

Band — δ / θ / mixed

✓Band agreement vs reports (0.74; default-mixed)
○Morphology/aperiodic features to sharpen band (FOOOF) — future

Localization

✓Lateralization L/R (focal-gated), overall + by band (4 curves), symmetry-augmented
✓Per-region one-vs-normal detection (temporal/frontal/central/parietal/occipital)
✓Focal lobe multi-class (honest; temporal reliable, posterior data-limited)
✓Generalized anterior-vs-posterior predominance (FIRDA/OIRDA)
○Region-stratified collection to lift posterior-lobe & θ-focal n

Sleep-stage specificity

✓Stage-stratified abnormal-vs-normal detection, by age (whole-head deviation)
○Stage-accentuation: which stage amplifies a case's pathological slowing

Description quality

✓Report-text agreement (region/side/band), gated per-axis
✓Example generated reports (focal & generalized, stage-aware)

Robustness / validation

✓Flip-consistency / left-right sign audit (=0)
✓Artifact-rejection validation (plan + local check)
○External / second-site validation (future)

Cohort & normative growth curves

The product itself: how normal EEG features vary with age, and how that differs by sleep stage.

Detection — age-dependent gate discrimination

How well the gate separates abnormal from normal, and where it is weaker (children).

Localization — lateralization (focal, L vs R)

Focal-gated, binary left-vs-right from signed band-matched asymmetry; symmetry-augmented (no left prior).

Localization — region

Two complementary views: per-region detectability vs normal, and the multi-class lobe confusion (honest).

Sleep-stage specificity

Does detection hold within each sleep stage, across age? (Secondary/robustness view.)

Tables & detail

Lateralization — band-matched detail

# Band-matched lateralization (focal, L vs R)

n=555 focal-lateralized. Rows = the case's reported band; columns = which band's asymmetry features the classifier used. AUROC for left-vs-right.

| case_band   |   n |   clf_delta |   clf_theta |   clf_both |
|:------------|----:|------------:|------------:|-----------:|
| delta       | 169 |       0.895 |       0.862 |      0.902 |
| theta       |  41 |       0.882 |       0.909 |      0.879 |
| mixed       | 317 |       0.877 |       0.834 |      0.87  |


- **Band-aware routed predictor** (delta→delta, theta→theta, mixed→both): overall AUROC **0.871**

- Delta-only-always baseline: AUROC 0.876


**Face validity:** on theta-predominant focal cases, the theta-asymmetry classifier should lateralize at least as well as the delta one — so the side we report is driven by the same band we call the slowing. (theta n is small; treat as indicative.)

Per-region detection (table)

# Per-region slowing detection (one-vs-normal)

For each region: AUROC separating recordings with slowing in that region from normal controls, using that region's age-adjusted slowing deviation (independent of other regions).

| region    |   n_pos |   auroc |    lo |    hi |
|:----------|--------:|--------:|------:|------:|
| temporal  |    2445 |   0.716 | 0.701 | 0.729 |
| frontal   |     691 |   0.674 | 0.651 | 0.696 |
| central   |     256 |   0.659 | 0.628 | 0.691 |
| parietal  |      66 |   0.742 | 0.682 | 0.806 |
| occipital |      67 |   0.753 | 0.692 | 0.804 |


_This is the clinically natural 'can we see region-X slowing at all?' question; unlike the multi-class lobe confusion it is not swamped by temporal predominance._

Region — focal lobe + generalized A/P (tables)

# Gated region localization (split by slowing type)


## (A) FOCAL-only lobe localization (n=1273) — acc 0.462, macro-F1 0.203

| region    |   precision |   recall |    f1 |    n |
|:----------|------------:|---------:|------:|-----:|
| temporal  |       0.872 |    0.501 | 0.636 | 1038 |
| frontal   |       0.171 |    0.342 | 0.228 |  149 |
| central   |       0.068 |    0.25  | 0.107 |   56 |
| parietal  |       0.026 |    0.13  | 0.043 |   23 |
| occipital |       0     |    0     | 0     |    7 |


## (B) GENERALIZED: anterior (FIRDA-like) vs posterior (OIRDA-like) predominance

- n=645 (anterior/frontal 542, posterior 103)

- anterior-minus-posterior delta gradient: AUROC **0.573**

- supervised LR on channel deviations: AUROC **0.658**


_For generalized slowing, side is undefined but A-P predominance is a real, reportable axis (frontal-predominant vs posterior-predominant intermittent rhythmic delta)._

Our features vs Morgoth (agreement)

# LR-on-deviations vs Morgoth
n=12379, 97 age/sex-adjusted feature@region deviations.

## vs Morgoth **p_abnormal**
- Discrimination vs clinical label: **our-LR AUC 0.962** | Morgoth AUC 0.921
- Agreement of probabilities: **Pearson r=0.693**, Spearman ρ=0.690
- Distillation (our features → Morgoth prob): **R²=0.456**

## vs Morgoth **p_slowing**
- Discrimination vs clinical label: **our-LR AUC 0.962** | Morgoth AUC 0.936
- Agreement of probabilities: **Pearson r=0.635**, Spearman ρ=0.694
- Distillation (our features → Morgoth prob): **R²=0.458**

Description agreement with clinical reports

# Agreement with clinical reports (report-derived finding flags)
Matched **11339 / 12379** cohort recordings to a report (EEG/HEEDB_Metadata). AUC of each score vs the report flag.

| target (report flag) | Morgoth AUC | our-LR AUC | our-simple AUC |
|---|---|---|---|
| r_abnormal (pos 0.45) | 0.904 | 0.751 | 0.668 |
| r_focal (pos 0.16) | 0.785 | 0.631 | 0.631 |
| r_gen (pos 0.66) | 0.745 | 0.647 | 0.603 |

**Read:** Morgoth tracks the reports strongly (abnormal ~0.90; focal ~0.79; generalized ~0.75). Our objective LR on age/sex deviations is close behind — face validity that our features capture what experts write. Band (δ/θ/mixed) and exact side/region need the report TEXT (scripts/18 part B).

## Part B — band/location from report TEXT (source: Box Brandon - PHI/Datasets/BDSP_deID/I0001-MGB/data_Unstructured/EEG_Reports_OtherSourceFiles/EEGs_And_Reports.csv)

## band/location agreement (our generated statement vs report), where report states it
- band: agreement 0.737 on n=5196
- side: agreement 0.785 on n=5406
- region: agreement 0.916 on n=407
